source: AWS EC2 on-demand pricing via instances.vantage.sh, which mirrors the AWS Price List API

aws_claims.cost_vs_x86: AWS Graviton-based instances cost up to 20% less than comparable x86-based Amazon EC2 instances.
aws_claims.energy_vs_x86: AWS Graviton-based instances use up to 60% less energy than comparable EC2 instances for the same performance.

method: Single-stream (batch size 1) cost, the regime this study measures. $/1M tokens = usd_per_hour * 1e6 / (tokens_per_sec * 3600). Prefill and decode are billed at the same hourly rate but reported separately because their throughput differs. A serverless GPU $/1M quote reflects a batched, saturated regime and is not directly comparable to these single-stream numbers.
serverless_gpu_reference: Hosted small-model (about 8B) serverless endpoints were around $0.10 to $0.24 per 1M tokens as of mid-2026 (secondary aggregators, batched regime), for order-of-magnitude context only.
